  "projects": [
    {
      "title": "Interdisciplinary Project: Disaster Management (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "Every student must undertake one project on Disaster Management. Objectives include creating awareness, preparing to face situations, ensuring participation in risk reduction plans.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Interdisciplinary Project: Forest Society and Colonialism (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "Compare the forest situations prevailed in pre-colonial, colonial and post-colonial era. Evaluate growth & role of commercial forestry.",
      "related_unit": "Livelihoods, Economies and Societies",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter IV Forest Society and Colonialism"
    },
    {
      "title": "Interdisciplinary Project: Natural Vegetation (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "To devise ways to protect the forest vegetation and wildlife in India.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": "Chapter 5 Natural Vegetation and Wildlife"
    },
    {
      "title": "Interdisciplinary Project: Consumer Rights / Social Issues (Class X)",
      "source_text": "Every student must undertake one project on Consumer Awareness OR Social Issues OR Sustainable Development.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Interdisciplinary Project: Globalisation and Lifelines of Economy (Class X)",
      "source_text": "Analyse the implication of globalisation for local economies. Discuss how globalisation is experienced differently.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "assessment_information": {
    "marks_distribution": [
      "Class IX History: 20 Marks (18+2 map pointing)",
      "Class IX Geography: 20 Marks (17+3 map marking)",
      "Class IX Political Science: 20 Marks",
      "Class IX Economics: 20 Marks",
      "Class X History: 20 Marks (18+2 map pointing)",
      "Class X Geography: 20 Marks (17+3 map pointing)",
      "Class X Political Science: 20 Marks",
      "Class X Economics: 20 Marks",
      "Internal Assessment Class IX/X: 20 Marks total (Periodic Test 5, Multiple Assessment 5, Subject Enrichment/Project Work 5, Portfolio 5)",
      "Board Exam Question Paper Design: MCQs 20 marks, Long Answer Questions (2-3-4-5 marks) totaling 60 marks, Map Pointing 5 marks"
    ],
    "excluded_or_non_assessed_content": [
      "Chapter 1 The Story of Village Palampur - assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only",
      "Chapter 5 Pastoralists in the Modern World (Class IX) - assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only",
      "Natural Vegetation and Wildlife map pointing (Class IX) - Only map pointing to be evaluated in annual examination",
      "The Age of Industrialisation (Class X) - To be assessed as part of Periodic Assessment only",
      "Lifelines of National Economy map pointing (Class X) - Only map pointing to be evaluated in Board Examination"
    ],
    "teacher_notes": [
      "Interdisciplinary projects must be carried out at school between April and September. Carryover of project to home is strictly avoided.",
      "Projects should use eco-friendly products without incurring too much expenditure.",
      "Project records (internal assessment) must be kept for three months from the date of declaration of result for verification.",
      "For Class IX, Project Work on Disaster Management requires collaboration with local authorities like Disaster Management Authorities, Fire Service, Police, etc.",
      "Map work should use maps available in the website of Govt. of India.",
      "Project reports can be handwritten or digital and may culminate in PPT/Exhibition/Skit/etc."
    ]
  }
}
